channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Estonia steps up patrols in the icy Baltic Sea in a show of force after suspected cable sabotage
item.description: ABOARD THE EML SAKALA IN THE BALTIC SEA (AP) — As they plied the gray, icy waters of the Baltic Sea west of Russia on Thursday, the crew of the Estonian minehunter EML Sakala kept a careful eye on any vessels slowing down suspiciously or suddenly changing course.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and HENDRIK OSULA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LA area's 2 biggest blazes burn at least 10,000 structures, while new fire leads to more evacuations
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area have burned at least 10,000 homes, buildings and other structures, officials said Thursday as they urged more people to heed evacuation orders after a new blaze ignited and quickly grew.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about thousands of evacuations and homes burned in Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — At least 10 people were killed and thousands of structures were burned as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What ignited the deadly California wildfires? Investigators consider an array of possibilities
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Investigators are considering an array of possible ignition sources for the huge fires that have killed at least 10 people and destroyed thousands of homes and businesses in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Winter storm plods into the Deep South, prompting states of emergency and school closures
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma lumbered eastward into southern U.S. states overnight, prompting governors to declare states of emergency and shuttering schools across the region. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New LA-area fire prompts more evacuations while over 10,000 structures lost to the 2 biggest blazes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area have burned at least 10,000 homes, buildings and other structures, officials said Thursday as they urged more people to heed evacuation orders after a new blaze ignited and quickly grew.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Lebanon's parliament elects army commander Joseph Aoun as president, ending a 2-year deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s parliament voted Thursday to elect army commander Joseph Aoun as head of state, filling a more than two-year-long presidential vacuum.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Los Angeles landmarks from film, TV damaged by wildfires
item.description: The high school where Brian De Palma brought Stephen King’s “Carrie” to life, Will Rogers’ ranch house, and a motel owned by William Randolph Hearst are among some of the famous structures that have been damaged or destroyed by the California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Wildfires latest: At least 10,000 structures have burned in a series of fires around Los Angeles
item.description: Firefighters battled Thursday to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed seven people, burned at least 10,000 structures from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In Los Angeles, people snap up air filters and wear masks against smoke pollution
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — With levels of sooty air pollution far too high across swaths of the Los Angeles area, Dana Benton is sleeping with a mask on, even with her air purifier running.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Apalachee High School student arrested for allegedly bringing gun to campus months after deadly mass shooting
item.description: Just four months after a devastating mass shooting at Georgia's Apalachee High School, another 14-year-old student brought a gun to campus, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google Trends: Nearly every US state is searching the Broncos over the Bills
item.description: We looked at Google Trends statistics to see whether the Broncos or the Bills were the talk of the country – and one team won by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KRON4

item.title: From Carter's funeral, an American snapshot: A singular image, 5 presidents, 379 years of history
item.description: Look at their faces — formal, etched with experience, laden with the weight of momentous decisions. Add up their years: 379 to be exact, enough time to take you back into the mid-1600s, when the notion of the American nation was still more than a century away.
item.dc:creator: TED ANTHONY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan woman gets jail after boy brought pink gun to school
item.description: A woman who illegally possessed a pink handgun that a child took to a Grand Rapids elementary school in a backpack has been sentenced to a year in jail.
item.dc:creator: John Hogan, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tells GOP he wants to write 100 executive orders
item.description: “He threw that out — 100 — there could be like 100 EOs, yeah. I believe him.” Sen. Kevin Cramer (R-N.D.) told The Hill, referring to Trump.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit by New Orleans truck attack victims says city, contractors failed to implement safety system
item.description: Six people who were injured and the father of a man who was killed in the New Year's truck attack filed a lawsuit Thursday against the City of New Orleans and two contractors, claiming they failed to protect revelers from an Army veteran who sped around a police blockade and raced down Bourbon Stree
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Letter from Gov. Cox calls for immediate removal of Salt Lake City ICE director
item.description: Gov. Cox signed a letter requesting changes to local immigration standards and the immediate removal of the ICE director in Salt Lake City.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires leave hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: An outage map provided by the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) showed just how widespread the outages extended
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: How to donate to LA wildfire relief
item.description: A swarm of winter wildfires ravaged America's most heavily-populated county to start 2025, scorching cars, banks, bungalows and multi-million dollar mansions.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KRON4

item.title: Risk not 'high enough' to warrant EPA cleanup in Tijuana River Valley
item.description: "The reality is that things have gotten much worse since then," she said. "Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: US warns of risk from most of world’s online pharmacies
item.description: The office of the U.S. Trade Representative warned that there’s a risk of ineffective or dangerous drugs from online pharmacies that are being illegally run.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: All 5 living presidents, political dignitaries attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Political leaders gathered to honor the life of former President Jimmy Carter during his state funeral at the Washington National Cathedral on Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the U.S., at least four in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: House Republican bill would pave way for Trump to acquire Panama Canal for US
item.description: Rep. Dusty Johnson (R-S.D.) will introduce a bill that would authorize the president to purchase the Panama Canal and put it under U.S. control, an acquisition that President-elect Trump has been pushing over the last several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Gingrich predicts Johnson will be Speaker for 6 years
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) is predicting that Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will remain in the top job for six years.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: How to safely evacuate during a wildfire
item.description: Evacuating a wildfire area can be scary, but experts say people can significantly improve their own safety with a little preparation and common sense.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blue states called out by women's group for ignoring risks posed by transgender inmates
item.description: An Independent Women study released Thursday highlights the dangers of housing biological male felons who identify as females at female-only prisons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for humility and service in Washington before being laid to rest in Georgia
item.description: Former U.S. President Jimmy Carter was honored with the pageantry of a funeral in the nation’s capital before a second service in his tiny Georgia hometown that launched a Depression-era farm boy to the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Watch: Reporter douses flames endangering California home on live TV
item.description: While covering one of the multiple fires currently ablaze in California, one reporter — a former EMT and volunteer firefighter — stepped in to stop flames from spreading to a home.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation
